question_html: Ômy›`i gv‡ÎiB GKUv AvKl©Y kw³ Av‡QÕ-GB ev‡K¨ Ômy›`iÕ kãwU †Kvb c`?

Choose the right option to fill in the gap in the following sentence.

‘The family ____ divided in their opinions.’
A synonym for ‘Frustrate’ is-
Choose the correct spelling.
Rare ____ the moments of pure joy.
To ensure the project stays on track, we must not deviate____ the original plan.
Among these sentences, which one has a causative verb?
options_html: The headmaster rises early in the morning.
The headmaster raises the flag every day.
He learns how to manage the committee.
The students remember their teacher’s birthday.
correct_answer_html: The headmaster raises the flag every day.

question_html: Complete the sentence: If we leave now, ________.
Which of the following is a causative verb?
Among these sentences, which one follows the rule of the second conditional?
options_html: If I had more time, I would learn to play the piano.
If it rains, I will bring an umbrella.
If you study hard, you’ll pass the exam.
If you went there, you may meet Kajol.
correct_answer_html: If I had more time, I would learn to play the piano.

question_html: The teacher let us ___ early today.
Even though she looked _____, she spoke ______.
The idiom ‘to drive home’ means-
options_html: To return to place of rest

question_html: The indirect speech of : He said to her, "What a cold day!"
options_html: He told her that it was a cold day.
He exclaimed sadly that it was a cold day.
He exclaimed sorrowfully that it was a cold day.
He exclaimed that it was a very cold day.
correct_answer_html: He exclaimed that it was a very cold day.

question_html: Make it simple: “The question was so difficult that I couldn't answer it.”
options_html: The question is too difficult to answer.
The question is so difficult for me to answer.
The question is so difficult to answer.
The question is too difficult for me to answer.
correct_answer_html: The question is too difficult for me to answer.

question_html: "The book reads well"- which one of the following sentences is the passive form of that sentence?
options_html: Well is read by the book.
The book is well when it is read.
correct_answer_html: The book is well when it is read.
